name: Green Beans with Sesame Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pan of water to a rolling boil and add the tablespoon of salt.
2. Add the green beans to the boiling water and cook for 2-5 minutes until just tender but still crisp.
3. Drain the beans thoroughly in a colander.
4. Quickly plunge the drained beans into cold water to set the color, then drain again.
5. In a small cast-iron frying pan over medium-high heat, dry roast the sesame seeds for a few minutes until roasted and nutty.
6. Reserve about 1/2 tablespoon of the toasted sesame seeds for garnish.
7. Grind the remaining sesame seeds in a clean coffee grinder or food processor.
8. In a bowl, combine the ground sesame seeds with soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, sugar, and 1 tablespoon of chicken stock from the beans.
9. Mix well until all ingredients are incorporated.
10. Drain the beans thoroughly (save the stock for another use).
11. Transfer the beans to a bowl and add the dressing, mixing gently to coat.
12. To serve Japanese-style, place the beans in small bowls or plates in a hill shape.
13. Sprinkle the reserved toasted sesame seeds over each serving.
